steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Small tanks hold 1 fish each, so $s$ small tanks hold $s \cdot 1 = s$ fish. Large tanks hold 2 fish each, so $b$ large tanks hold $b \cdot 2 = 2b$ fish.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: The total number of fish is the sum from both tank types: $s + 2b = 37$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
Evaluate each choice:
  • $s + 2b = 37$ $s + 2b = 37$. Each small tank contributes 1 fish and each large tank contributes 2 fish, matching the setup exactly. That is the correct answer.
  • $s + b = 37$ $s + b = 37$. This would mean every tank, regardless of size, holds exactly 1 fish. Incorrect.
  • $2s + b = 37$ $2s + b = 37$. This reverses the capacities, assigning 2 fish to small tanks and 1 fish to large tanks. Incorrect.
  • $2s + 2b = 37$ $2s + 2b = 37$. This assumes every tank holds 2 fish, ignoring the small-tank capacity of 1. Incorrect.
